reasoning_messages.content:
**Remediation applied**

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Created `/etc/audit/rules.d/lremovexattr.rules` | Added four audit lines (b32/b64, user & root) |
| Set file mode | `0640` (owner read/write, group read) |
| No duplicate lines or unintended changes | File contains only the required rules |

**Contents of `/etc/audit/rules.d/lremovexattr.rules`**

```
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lremovexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lremovexattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lremovexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lremovexattr -F auid=0 -F key=perm_mod
```

The audit daemon will load these rules on its next restart/reload, ensuring that all `lremovexattr` syscalls are audited for both regular users and root as required. No SSH or firewall settings were modified.
